issues.comment: forEach with async callback — promises silently discarded. Array.prototype.forEach ignores the return value of callbacks, so when the callback is async, the returned Promise is silently discarded. The await getCalendar(...) and subsequent deleteEvent/deleteMeeting calls inside the callback will not be awaited by the calling code. The function proceeds to the next statements (sending emails, etc.) before the calendar/video deletion operations even start. Errors are uncatchable as rejected promises become unhandled promise rejections. This is a regression from the old code. Correct fix: Use Promise.all(bookingRefsFiltered.map(async (bookingRef) => { ... })) or a for...of loop instead of forEach.

forEach with async callback — promises silently discarded. Array.prototype.forEach ignores the return value of callbacks, so when the callback is async, the returned Promise is silently discarded. The await getCalendar(...) and subsequent deleteEvent/deleteMeeting calls inside the callback will not be awaited. The forEach is wrapped in a try/catch block, but since forEach doesn't await the async callbacks, rejected promises will become unhandled promise rejections instead of being caught. This is a regression from the old code. Correct fix: Use Promise.all(bookingRefsFiltered.map(async (bookingRef) => { ... })) or a for...of loop instead of forEach.

forEach with async callback — promises silently discarded. Array.prototype.forEach ignores the return value of callbacks, so when the callback is async, the returned Promise is silently discarded. The await getCalendar(...) and subsequent deleteEvent/deleteMeeting calls inside the callback will not be awaited. The forEach is wrapped in a try/catch block, but since forEach doesn't await the async callbacks, rejected promises will become unhandled promise rejections instead of being caught. This is a regression from the old code. Correct fix: Use Promise.all(bookingRefsFiltered.map(async (bookingRef) => { ... })) or a for...of loop instead of forEach.

getCalendarCredentials stores unawaited Promise, breaking the type contract. getCalendarCredentials remains a synchronous function but calls getCalendar(credential) which is now async (returns Promise<Calendar | null>). The calendar field stored in the returned objects is now a Promise<Calendar | null> instead of Calendar | null. While getConnectedCalendars handles this by awaiting the stored promise, other consumers of getCalendarCredentials may access the calendar property expecting a resolved value without await, leading to type errors or incorrect behavior.
